articles.headline: UK Society of Authors launches logo to identify books written by humans not AI
articles.description: Tracy Chevalier announces registration scheme at the London Book Fair as AI works flood marketThe Society of Authors (SoA) has launched a scheme to help identify works written by humans in a market increasingly flooded by AI-generated books.The scheme is the first of its kind launched by a UK trade association, and allows authors to register their books and download a “Human Authored” logo to display on their back cover. Continue reading...

articles.headline: ChatGPT now explains math and physics with interactive visualizations
articles.description: ChatGPT now displays interactive visualizations for math and physics topics where users can tweak variables and watch graphs update in real time. More than 70 concepts are available at launch. The article ChatGPT now explains math and physics with interactive visualizations appeared first on The Decoder.

software_engineering.headline: So, a new logo wants to tell you if a book's human-written
software_engineering.what_changed: Basically, the UK's Society of Authors just launched a new 'Human Authored' logo. Authors can register their books and put this logo on the back cover to show it wasn't written by AI, aiming to stand out in a market increasingly full of AI-generated books.
software_engineering.why_care: For you, this is a pretty interesting move in the broader conversation about AI-generated content. While it's about books now, you can bet similar ideas might pop up for code or other creative outputs down the line.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Keep an eye on this: It's a sign of how industries are starting to push back against AI-generated content. You might see similar 'authenticity' labels pop up in other creative fields, maybe even for code or documentation, as AI gets more sophisticated.

software_engineering.headline: ChatGPT now visualizes math and physics concepts.
software_engineering.what_changed: ChatGPT now offers interactive math and physics visualizations.
software_engineering.why_care: Bottom line: AI tools now offer interactive learning experiences.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Keep an eye on this: AI models are expanding into interactive educational tools.

data_ai.headline: Turns out, a new 'Human Authored' logo is coming for books
data_ai.what_changed: Basically, the UK's Society of Authors (SoA) just launched a new 'Human Authored' logo. Authors can register their books and put this logo on the cover to show it was written by a human, not AI. It's their way of tackling the surge of AI-generated books out there.
data_ai.why_care: For you, this really shows how much people are thinking about content authenticity as AI gets better at generating text. It's not just books; you might see similar pushes for transparency in data documentation, code, or even generated reports as AI becomes more common in those areas too.
data_ai.what_to_do: The bigger picture: This scheme is a pretty clear indicator of how industries are starting to react to the rise of generative AI. We'll likely see more of these 'authenticity' or 'human-verified' initiatives popping up across various content types as AI becomes more sophisticated.

marketing.headline: So, there's a new 'Human Authored' logo for books
marketing.what_changed: Basically, the UK Society of Authors (SoA) just rolled out a new scheme where authors can register their books and put a 'Human Authored' logo on the cover. It's their way of helping readers tell the difference between books written by people and those made by AI, especially since there are so many AI-generated ones flooding the market now.
marketing.why_care: For you, this is a big deal if you're creating content or managing brands. It highlights a growing consumer need for authenticity and transparency. People want to know if the content they're consuming, whether it's a book, an article, or a social media post, came from a human. This could totally change how you think about 'human-verified' content in your own marketing efforts.
marketing.what_to_do: Try thinking about how you could apply a similar 'human-verified' concept to your own content. Are there ways to clearly signal to your audience that your brand's content is genuinely created by humans, not just AI? It's all about building trust.

cybersecurity.headline: So, authors are getting a "Human Authored" logo for their books.
cybersecurity.what_changed: Basically, the UK Society of Authors just launched a scheme where human writers can register their books and get a special "Human Authored" logo. It's meant to help readers spot real books in a market that's getting swamped with AI-generated stuff.
cybersecurity.why_care: For you, this isn't directly about your daily incident response, but it highlights a growing problem: how do you verify the origin and authenticity of digital content? We're seeing this in books now, but it's a bigger challenge for everything from news articles to code, and it's a trust issue that security professionals will increasingly face.
cybersecurity.what_to_do: The bigger picture: This "Human Authored" logo is an early attempt to establish provenance in a world where AI can generate convincing fakes. Think about what this means for verifying the integrity of documentation, code, or even threat intelligence reports in the future.
